info.title: NYC — Programmable City (unified surface)
info.summary: Every NYC government API in one design-first contract — 752 operations across 70 agencies, each mapped to an MCP tool and a common government-process Agent Skill.
info.description: A unified, design-first OpenAPI across all 67 assessed NYC government domains, generated by scripts/build-experience.py from each agency's own OpenAPI. Paths are namespaced by agency slug; every operation carries x-mcp-tool (its MCP tool) and x-agent-skill (one of ten common government-process skills). This is the machine artifact behind the single-page documentation at https://nyc.apievangelist.com/experience.html. Design-first — not a deployed gateway. Full per-operation detail lives in each agency's source OpenAPI (x-source-openapi).

servers.description: Static reference API — example data at stable URLs, reads only (GitHub Pages).

referenceApi.note: Example data synthesized from the agency schemas; GET-callable, reads only.

prompts.name: who_represents_this_address
prompts.description: Given a NYC address, return the council member, community board, borough, and every district that represents it.

prompts.name: everything_about_this_place
prompts.description: Everything the city knows about a BBL/BIN/address across agencies — buildings, housing, environment, complaints, landmarks.

prompts.name: how_do_i_apply_for
prompts.description: Explain how to apply for a given permit, license, or benefit and start the application with the owning agency.

prompts.name: report_a_problem_at
prompts.description: File a 311-style report or complaint for a location and route it to the owning agency.

prompts.name: whats_the_status_of_my
prompts.description: Look up the status of an application, permit, complaint, or case across agencies by its id.

prompts.name: what_agency_handles
prompts.description: Identify which NYC agency owns a given task or record and hand off to its tools.

prompts.name: find_a_service_near_me
prompts.description: Find city services or facilities near a location (parks, libraries, clinics, offices).

resources.name: NYC API catalog
resources.description: All 67 agency APIs with their OpenAPI, MCP server, and Agent Skills.

resources.name: nyc-commons geography spine
resources.description: The shared Borough / BBL / BIN / district vocabulary every place resolves to.

resources.name: Citizen-transaction taxonomy
resources.description: The common government processes (Apply, Report, Request-records, Schedule, Register, Dispute, Pay) and the domains behind each.

resources.name: Cross-agency key registry
resources.description: The join keys (BBL, BIN, council district, ...) that link one agency's records to another's.

resources.name: Government-process skills index
resources.description: The ten common government-process Agent Skills and the operations each orchestrates.

resources.name: Resolved place (templated)
resources.description: A nyc-commons Place for a BBL — its geography spine plus the agencies keyed on it.

resources.name: Agency profile (templated)
resources.description: One agency's API + MCP + skills profile.
